about.description: Zog is a free streaming platform for movies and shows at zog.watch.
about.help: Can't find an answer? <0>Get support here.</0>

q1.title: Where does the content come from?
q1.body: Zog does not host any content. When you click on something to watch, the internet is searched for the selected media (On the loading screen and in the 'video sources' tab you can see which source you're using). Media never gets uploaded by Zog, everything is through this searching mechanism.

q2.title: Where can I request a show or movie?
q2.body: It's not possible to request a show or movie, Zog does not manage any content. All content is viewed through sources on the internet.

q3.title: The search results display the show or movie, why can't I play it?
q3.body: Our search results are powered by The Movie Database (TMDB) and display regardless of whether our sources actually have the content.

q4.title: What about my data and stuff?
q4.body: All data is synced to the community backend, anyone is free to use this as well.

q5.title: How can I find out more?
q5.body: Visit the Support page or email the address at the bottom of this site.

q7.title: How do I make an account?
q7.body: Tap "Sync to Cloud" in the dropdown menu or go to Settings to create and sync a community account.

q9.title: This media is missing / Can I request media?
q9.body: No—since we don't host content, we cannot add or change what's available. The content list is based on what's publicly accessible through the search mechanism.

q10.title: I can't find the movie/show I'm searching for!
q10.body: TMDB powers our search. Try tapping the 🔎 icon for advanced search options if results aren't accurate.

q11.title: Why am I seeing a black screen?
q11.body: Likely a corrupt or broken video file. Try switching sources.

q12.title: The episode I'm watching is incorrect.
q12.body: Switch to a different source. We don't control any content or episode accuracy.

q13.title: The video quality isn't what I expected.
q13.body: Try switching sources. Quality varies by provider; FED API often offers more options.

q14.title: 4K isn't available.
q14.body: If 4K is not shown, it's likely unavailable from that source.

q15.title: Why is there no audio?
q15.body: Ensure your device isn't muted and try switching sources. Audio issues may be source-specific.

q16.title: The video is tinted or washed out.
q16.body: Could be a faulty file or an HDR video. Ensure your device supports HDR, or switch sources.

q17.title: I'm experiencing visual glitches.
q17.body: Visual bugs usually come from poor file uploads. Try switching sources.

q18.title: The source is loading slowly.
q18.body: Speed depends on the hosting site. Switch to another source for better performance.

q19.title: How do I switch sources?
q19.body: While playing, tap the ⚙️ icon, then choose another source under "video sources".

q20.title: What is Finger API?
q20.body: Finger API is our private scraper that pulls streams directly from Febbox.

q21.title: Does Finger API work on all devices?

q22.title: Why do I need the extension or custom proxy?
q22.body: Some sources block scrapers. The extension acts like a local proxy and bypasses such blocks using your IP, headers, and cookies.

q23.title: What is the extension? Is it safe?
q23.body: It's a local proxy tool used to access streams that restrict scrapers. It mimics your browsing session. It's also completly opensource and can be found on our github.

q24.title: My febbox token fails during setup. What should I do?
q24.body: Your ISP might be blocking FED API. Use a VPN to bypass the block, we recommend Cloudflare WARP because it's free and easy to use. You can also change your region in the admin panel.

q25.title: How do I change languages?
q25.body: We prioritize English, but some sources offer other languages. Try switching sources.

q26.title: My subtitles aren't in sync.
q26.body: Use the "Customize" option in subtitle settings or tap [ and ] on your keyboard to adjust. You can also upload your own subtitle file.

about.title: About Zog and FAQ (^▽^)

start.explainer: To get the best streams possible, you will need to choose which streaming method you want to use.

explainer.intro: Zog doesn't host videos. It relies on third-party websites for content, so you need to choose how it connects to those sites.
explainer.extensionDescription: The extension gives you access to the most sources. It acts as a local proxy and can handle sites that need special cookies or headers to load.
explainer.proxyDescription: The proxy scrapes media from other websites. It bypasses browser restrictions (like CORS) to allow scraping.
explainer.defaultDescription: Uses Zog's built-in proxy. It's the easiest option but might be slower due to shared bandwidth.

explainer.outro: If you have more questions, visit the Support page or email us.

skipTime.connectApiKeyMessage: To submit new segments, enter your TheIntroDB API key in the connections settings

modal.title: Submit Timestamps to TheIntroDB
modal.description: Contribute to TheIntroDB by submitting accurate segment timestamps. All submissions are accepted by the community before being published.

start.intro: 2:30 or 150 (leave empty for start at beginning)
start.recap: 2:30 or 150 (leave empty for start at beginning)

end.credits: 3:30 or 210 (leave empty for end of media)
end.preview: 3:30 or 210 (leave empty for end of media)

modal.whenToDesc: Enter timestamps in seconds (e.g. 150), mm:ss format (e.g. 2:30), or hh:mm:ss format (e.g. 1:42:20). We'll automatically convert the format for you.

guide.endDesc: Optional - empty if extends to end

intro.startDesc: Optional - leave empty if the intro starts at the beginning of the episode.
intro.endDesc: Required - when the intro ends (usually when the title card or main episode begins).
intro.durationDesc: Most intros are 30–90 seconds. Minimum 5s if both start and end are set.
intro.excludeDesc: Do not include cold opens or pre-intro scenes; only the main title sequence.

recap.startDesc: Optional - leave empty if the recap starts at the beginning of the episode.
recap.endDesc: Required - when the recap ends and the new content begins.
recap.durationDesc: Recaps vary in length. Minimum 5s if both start and end are set.
recap.excludeDesc: Only the "previously on" or recap segment; do not include the intro or new scenes.

credits.startDesc: Required - when the closing credits begin (e.g. when the first credit text appears).
credits.endDesc: Optional - leave empty if credits run to the end of the file.
credits.durationDesc: Minimum 5s if end is provided. Can extend to end of media.
credits.excludeDesc: Exclude mid-credits or post-credits scenes; only the main credit roll.

preview.startDesc: Required - when the preview starts (e.g. when the preview text appears).
preview.endDesc: Optional - leave empty if the preview ends at the end of the media.
preview.excludeDesc: Preview is usually 15-90 seconds at the end of the episode previewing the next episode.

error.tidbKey: TIDB API key is not set

support.title: Support
support.text: Zog is designed to be as user-friendly as possible. However, people still have questions and issues. This page is here to help resolve these shortcomings

q1.body: Email the address at the bottom of this page for help.
q1.title: Where can I get help?

q2.body: We have a <0>GitHub</0> where you can create a detailed issue in our repository. Additionally, if you wish, you can create a pull request to fix the issue yourself.
q2.title: How can I report a bug or issue?

pas.text: Zog was a dream and Pas made it a reality.

options.discoverDescription: Show the Discover section on the Homepage below your bookmarked media.
options.featuredDescription: Show a carousel of featured movies and shows at the top of your homepage.
options.modalDescription: Show the details modal when you click on a media card instead of going to the watch page.
options.logosDescription: Show image logos instead of text titles in the details modal and featured section.
options.logosNotice: Most of the time, logos are English only. Other languages might want to disable this!
options.carouselViewDescription: Display your currently watching and bookmark sections as carousels instead of a grid.
options.minimalCardsDescription: Hide text content (title, type, year) on media cards, showing only the poster image.
options.forceCompactEpisodeViewDescription: Make the episode carousel in the player to use the "classic" compact vertical view.
options.homeSectionOrderDescription: Drag and drop to reorder the watching and bookmarks sections on your homepage. Group order can be editied from the home page.

server.description: If you would like to connect to a custom backend to store your data, enable this and provide the URL. <0>Instructions.</0>
server.label: Backend Server
server.changeWarning: Changing backend server will log you out. Continue?

migration.description: <0>Migrate my data</0> to a new server.

server.notice: Your account does NOT sync between backends, so remember which one you use!

errorStatus.description: It seems that one or more items in this setup need your attention.
errorStatus.title: Something needs your attention.

setup.itemError: There is something wrong with this setting. Go through setup again to fix it.

successStatus.description: All things are in place for you to start watching your favorite media.
successStatus.title: Everything is set up!

unsetStatus.description: Please click the button to the right to start the setup process.
unsetStatus.title: You haven't gone through setup

workers.description: To make the application function, all traffic is routed through proxies. Enable this if you want to bring your own workers. <0>Instructions.</0>
workers.emptyState: No workers yet (｡•́︿•̀｡), add one below
workers.label: Use custom proxy workers

proxyTMDB.title: Proxy TMDB
proxyTMDB.description: Only needed if you can't access TheMovieDB directly, such as if your ISP blocks it. It is recomended to disable the Discover section to improve performance with this.

tidb.description: Contribute to TheIntroDB by leaving feedback on intro, recap, and credits segments. <0>Learn more.</0>

trakt.title: Trakt
trakt.description: Sync your watchlist and history with Trakt.
trakt.details: Syncing might take a few minutes to complete. Changes on Trakt may conflict with local changes, so local items are prioritized.

preferences.title: Preferences
preferences.autoSubtitleSyncDescription: Automatically aligns subtitles to the audio by analyzing speech while you watch, correcting out-of-sync subtitles without manual adjustment. May not work on every source.
preferences.languageDescription: Language applied to the entire application, only English has silly stuff.
preferences.thumbnailDescription: Most of the time, videos don't have thumbnails. You can enable this setting to generate them on the fly but they can make your video slower.
preferences.autoplayDescription: Automatically play the next episode in a series after reaching the end. Can be enabled by users with the browser extension, a custom proxy, or with the default setup if allowed by the host.
preferences.skipCreditsDescription: When enabled, automatically play the next episode at 99% completion to skip end credits. When disabled, wait until the episode is fully completed.
preferences.autoSkipSegmentsDescription: When enabled, automatically skip intro, recap, and preview segments while watching.
preferences.lowPerformanceModeDescription: Optimizes the application for slower connections and devices by disabling bandwidth-heavy features. This mode reduces data usage and improves performance while keeping the core search and watch functionality intact.
preferences.holdToBoostDescription: Hold spacebar or touch and hold the screen to temporarily increase playback speed to 2x. Release to return to previous speed.
preferences.doubleClickToSeekDescription: Double tap on the left or right side of the player to seek 10 seconds forward or backward.
preferences.keyboardShortcutsDescription: Customize the keyboard shortcuts for the application. Hold ` to show this help anytime
preferences.sourceOrderDescription:
Drag and drop to reorder sources. This will determine the order in which sources are checked for the media you are trying to watch. If a source is greyed out, it means the extension is required for that source.

(The default order is best for most users)
preferences.embedOrderDescription:
Drag and drop to reorder embeds. This will determine the order in which embeds are checked for the media you are trying to watch.

(The default order is best for most users)
preferences.manualSourceDescription: Require picking a source before scraping. Disables automatic source selection and opens the source picker when starting playback.
preferences.autoResumeOnPlaybackErrorDescription: Automatically continue searching for other sources when the current source fails during playback. If disabled, you'll see an error screen with a manual resume option.
preferences.lastSuccessfulSourceDescription: Automatically prioritize the source that successfully provided content for the previous episode. This helps ensure continuity when watching series.

subtitles.backgroundBlurEnabledDescription: Disabling caption background blur may fix some flickering issues
subtitles.previewQuote: Convinced life is meaningless, I lack the courage of my conviction.
subtitles.title: Subtitles

onboarding.title: Febbox token
onboarding.description: Bring your own FREE Febbox account to gain access to FED API, the best sources with 4K quality, Dolby Atmos, skip intro and the fastest load times! Highly recommended option!
onboarding.note: Note: FED API was a scraper originally created by OG Zog Developer Nemo. Zog recreated the scraper to match its functionalities — it does not take any credit for the name or the original scraper.

setup.title: To get your UI token:

step.1: 1. Go to <0>febbox.com</0> and log in with Google (use a fresh account!)
step.2: 2. Open DevTools or inspect the page
step.5: 5. Close the tab, but do NOT logout!

tokenExample.title: Example Token
tokenExample.description: This is what a Febbox UI token looks like:
tokenExample.warning: Don't try to use, it's fake.

setup.trafficExplanation: Febbox gives you high-speed traffic per month, the streams might buffer more after you've used up your quota. Depends on your internet speed and the quality of the stream.
setup.useMp4: Enable MP4 streams. May be faster outside of the U.S., but audio tracks cannot be changed.

status.invalid_token: Failed to fetch a 'VIP' stream. Your token is invalid!

debrid.title: Debrid (Beta)

debrid.notice: Notice: This may not work on all browsers, use Chromium for the best compatibility. If you hear no audio, use another source. :(

status.failure: Failed to connect to Debrid. Please check your API key.
status.api_down: Hmm, something went wrong. Please try again later.
status.invalid_token: Invalid API key or non-premium account. Debrid requires a premium account.
